 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-f8f","title":"EPIC: Phase 3 - Flywheel Integration","description":"## Overview
Phase 3 integrates with the full Flywheel ecosystem including Beads/BV, CASS, CM, UBS, and enables intelligent conflict resolution with AI assistance.

## Phase 3 Goal
Full integration with Flywheel ecosystem

## Key Deliverables

### Flywheel Tool Integration
- Beads/BV integration
  - BVClient for CRUD operations
  - Triage API (ready beads, blocked beads, triageWork)
  - Insights API (bottlenecks, keystones)
  - Galaxy View dependency visualization
  - Graph rendering with WebGL (react-force-graph)
- CASS search integration
  - Search across agent conversations
  - Session context retrieval
  - History indexing
- CM memory integration
  - Playbook rules retrieval
  - Context queries before tasks
  - Learning feedback loop
- UBS scanner integration
  - Scan before commit
  - Auto-bead creation for findings
  - Fix workflow integration

### Advanced Conflict Resolution
- Intelligent Conflict Resolution Assistant
  - AI-powered merge suggestions
  - Semantic understanding of changes
  - Auto-resolution rules
  - User preference learning

### Session Management
- First-Class Session Handoff Protocol
  - Structured context transfer
  - Resource handover
  - Pending work queue transfer
  - Handoff audit trail

### Collaboration Visualization
- Real-Time Agent Collaboration Graph
  - Live visualization of agent activity
  - File heat maps
  - Communication flow visualization
  - Interaction timeline

### Safety & Coordination
- SLB Safety Guardrails integration
  - Pre-flight checks
  - Approval workflows
  - Escalation paths
- Git coordination service
  - Branch management
  - Merge orchestration
  - Conflict prevention

### Fleet Management
- RU (Repo Updater) integration
  - Fleet status dashboard
  - Multi-repo sync status
  - Agent-sweep orchestration
  - Three-phase review workflow

### DCG Advanced Features
- Allowlist management UI
- False positive feedback loop
- Pack configuration UI
- Custom pattern editor

## Phase Completion Criteria
- [ ] BV triage returns ready work for agents
- [ ] Galaxy View renders dependency graph interactively
- [ ] CASS search finds relevant prior solutions
- [ ] CM provides context rules for tasks
- [ ] UBS findings create beads automatically
- [ ] Conflict Resolution Assistant suggests merges
- [ ] Session handoffs preserve full context
- [ ] Collaboration graph shows live agent activity
- [ ] RU fleet status visible in dashboard
- [ ] Agent-sweep runs through Gateway

## Testing Requirements
- Unit test coverage >80% for all clients
- Integration tests for all Flywheel tool APIs
- E2E tests for triage → work → complete cycle
- Visual regression tests for Galaxy View
- Performance tests for graph rendering (1000+ nodes)

### Logging
- [ ] Unit/integration tests emit structured logs with `correlationId` + relevant entity IDs (never secrets)
- [ ] E2E tests capture artifacts (trace/video/screenshot/console logs) and surface failure context per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`


## Success Criteria

- [ ] Full flywheel loop works: Beads/BV → Agent Mail coordination → Gateway execution → UBS scan → CASS index/search → CM rule extraction → context packs
- [ ] Intelligent conflict resolution produces actionable suggestions and logs rationale/audit trail
- [ ] First-class session handoff protocol transfers context + resources safely (with acknowledgements)
- [ ] Git coordination + RU orchestration can run end-to-end on a repo fleet (dry-run + execute modes)
- [ ] Tests: contract tests cover all integrated REST surfaces + E2E covers critical user workflows per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

## Reference

- `flywheel_gateway-y19` (embedded `docs/PLAN.md` spec snapshot)

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-f9d","title":"FEAT: Metrics and Alerts System","description":"## Overview

Comprehensive monitoring with configurable alerts for proactive issue detection. The Metrics and Alerts System provides real-time visibility into Flywheel Gateway operations, enabling operators to track agent performance, detect issues early, and maintain system health.

## Background & Reasoning

Without proper observability, operators are blind to:
- Agent performance degradation before users notice
- Resource exhaustion approaching critical thresholds
- Patterns indicating systemic issues
- Cost trends that could lead to budget overruns

The Metrics system captures key indicators across all subsystems while the Alerts system provides configurable notifications based on thresholds and conditions.

**Architecture Note:** Prometheus remains the **authoritative** source for real-time alerting and SLOs; ClickHouse is used for long-term analytics and historical aggregation (audit, usage, and event logs).

## Technical Architecture

### Metrics Data Model

```typescript
interface MetricSnapshot {
  timestamp: Date;

  // Agent metrics
  agents: {
    total: number;
    byStatus: Record<string, number>;
    byType: Record<string, number>;
  };

  // Token usage
  tokens: {
    last24h: number;
    last7d: number;
    last30d: number;
    byModel: Record<string, number>;
    trend: 'up' | 'down' | 'stable';
    trendPercent: number;
  };

  // Performance
  performance: {
    avgResponseMs: number;
    p50ResponseMs: number;
    p95ResponseMs: number;
    p99ResponseMs: number;
    successRate: number;
  };

  // Flywheel metrics
  flywheel: {
    beadsOpen: number;
    beadsClosed24h: number;
    conflictsDetected: number;
    conflictsResolved: number;
    reservationsActive: number;
    messagesExchanged24h: number;
  };

  // System metrics
  system: {
    wsConnections: number;
    apiLatencyMs: number;
    daemonsHealthy: number;
    daemonsTotal: number;
    memoryUsageMb: number;
    cpuPercent: number;
  };
}
```

### Alert Configuration

```typescript
type AlertSeverity = 'info' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'critical';

interface Alert {
  id: string;
  type: AlertType;
  severity: AlertSeverity;
  title: string;
  message: string;
  source: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  expiresAt?: Date;
  acknowledged: boolean;
  acknowledgedAt?: Date;
  acknowledgedBy?: string;
  actions?: AlertAction[];
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
}

type AlertType =
  | 'agent_error'
  | 'agent_stalled'
  | 'conflict_detected'
  | 'reservation_expired'
  | 'daemon_failed'
  | 'quota_warning'
  | 'quota_exceeded'
  | 'approval_required'
  | 'security_violation'
  | 'system_health';

interface AlertRule {
  name: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  condition: (context: AlertContext) => boolean;
  severity: AlertSeverity;
  title: string | ((context: AlertContext) => string);
  message: string | ((context: AlertContext) => string);
  cooldown?: number;  // Minimum time between alerts
  actions?: AlertAction[];
}
```

### Default Alert Rules

- **agent_stalled**: Agent no output for 5+ minutes → warning
- **quota_warning**: Account at 80%+ quota → warning
- **quota_exceeded**: Account at 100% quota → error
- **daemon_failed**: Daemon process failed → critical
- **conflict_detected**: File conflict requires resolution → info
- **security_violation**: Safety rule triggered → error

### REST API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/metrics` | Current metrics snapshot |
| `GET` | `/metrics/history` | Historical metrics |
| `GET` | `/metrics/compare` | Compare time periods |
| `POST` | `/metrics/snapshot` | Create named snapshot |
| `GET` | `/metrics/snapshots` | List snapshots |
| `POST` | `/metrics/export` | Export metrics data |
| `GET` | `/alerts` | List active alerts |
| `GET` | `/alerts/history` | Alert history |
| `POST` | `/alerts/{id}/acknowledge` | Acknowledge alert |
| `POST` | `/alerts/{id}/dismiss` | Dismiss alert |
| `POST` | `/alerts/{id}/action` | Execute alert action |
| `GET` | `/alerts/rules` | List alert rules |
| `PUT` | `/alerts/rules` | Update alert rules |
| `GET` | `/health/prometheus` | Prometheus metrics endpoint |

### OpenTelemetry Integration

```typescript
// Metric types
const agentCounter = meter.createCounter('flywheel.agents.spawned');
const tokenHistogram = meter.createHistogram('flywheel.tokens.used');
const latencyHistogram = meter.createHistogram('flywheel.api.latency');
const activeGauge = meter.createObservableGauge('flywheel.agents.active');
```

## File Locations

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Metrics Service | `apps/gateway/src/services/metrics.service.ts` |
| Alert Service | `apps/gateway/src/services/alert.service.ts` |
| Alert Rules | `apps/gateway/src/services/alert-rules.ts` |
| Metrics Routes | `apps/gateway/src/routes/metrics.routes.ts` |
| Alert Routes | `apps/gateway/src/routes/alerts.routes.ts` |
| Prometheus Endpoint | `apps/gateway/src/routes/health.routes.ts` |
| Metrics Dashboard UI | `apps/web/src/components/analytics/MetricsDashboard.tsx` |
| Alert Center UI | `apps/web/src/components/analytics/AlertCenter.tsx` |

## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] MetricsService.record() creates correct metric types (counter, gauge, histogram)
- [ ] AlertRule.evaluate() returns correct state for each condition type
- [ ] Alert deduplication works for repeated violations within cooldown
- [ ] Histogram bucket boundaries are correctly configured
- [ ] Trend calculation produces accurate up/down/stable values

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Metrics are persisted and queryable via REST API
- [ ] Alert triggers create database records correctly
- [ ] Alert acknowledgment updates state and records user
- [ ] Metric aggregation returns correct summaries
- [ ] Prometheus endpoint exports valid metrics format
- [ ] OpenTelemetry spans correlate with metrics

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Grafana dashboard shows live metrics (via Prometheus)
- [ ] Dashboard refreshes in real-time via WebSocket
- [ ] Alert visible in UI with correct severity styling
- [ ] Alert can be acknowledged with optional comment
- [ ] Historical metrics queryable and graphable

### Load Tests
- [ ] System handles 10,000 metric points/second
- [ ] P95 metric recording latency < 1ms
- [ ] Alert evaluation completes within 100ms for 1000 rules
- [ ] Prometheus scrape endpoint returns within 500ms

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Metric recording continues if database is slow (buffered)
- [ ] Alerts fire even if notification delivery fails (logged)
- [ ] Invalid metric name produces validation error
- [ ] Alert loop is debounced by cooldown mechanism

### Logging
- [ ] Metrics/alerts tests log `metricName`, label set, thresholds, and `alertId` with correlation IDs
- [ ] Notification triggers in tests log routing decisions without exposing secrets


## Logging Requirements

### Metric Recording
```typescript
logger.debug('metrics:recorded', {
  correlationId,
  metricName,
  value,
  labels,
  timestamp
});
```

### Alert Firing
```typescript
logger.info('alert:fired', {
  correlationId,
  alertId,
  type,
  severity,
  source,
  message
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Metrics model + storage implemented for core dimensions (agents, jobs, WS, scans, reservations)
- [ ] Alert rules can be created/updated/disabled and evaluate deterministically (with debounce/cooldowns)
- [ ] REST endpoints return consistent envelopes + correlation IDs; errors use shared taxonomy
- [ ] WebSocket emits metric/alert events using the standard event envelope and supports replay/cursor resume
- [ ] UI dashboard renders key charts + alert list, with clear empty/error states
- [ ] Structured logging ties alerts/metrics to correlation IDs and relevant entity IDs
- [ ] Prometheus endpoint exports all key metrics in standard format
- [ ] OpenTelemetry integration provides distributed tracing context

## References

- PLAN.md §21 - Metrics & Alerts System
- OpenTelemetry Metrics Specification
- Prometheus Best Practices","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Metric: counter increments correctly
- [ ] Metric: gauge sets value
- [ ] Metric: histogram records distribution
- [ ] Metric: labels attached correctly
- [ ] OpenTelemetry: span created
- [ ] OpenTelemetry: span attributes set
- [ ] OpenTelemetry: span parent linked
- [ ] Alert rule: condition evaluation
- [ ] Alert rule: threshold comparison
- [ ] Alert rule: duration requirement
- [ ] Alert notification: formats message
- [ ] Alert notification: routes to channel

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Prometheus endpoint exports metrics
- [ ] Metrics include correct labels
- [ ] Histograms have correct buckets
- [ ] Traces correlate with logs
- [ ] Alert fires when threshold exceeded
- [ ] Alert resolves when condition clears
- [ ] Notification delivered to Slack/email

### E2E Tests
- [ ] Grafana dashboard shows metrics
- [ ] Dashboard refreshes in real-time
- [ ] Alert visible in UI
- [ ] Alert can be acknowledged
- [ ] Historical metrics queryable

### Performance Tests
- [ ] Metric recording <1ms
- [ ] Prometheus scrape <500ms
- [ ] 10k metrics exported efficiently
- [ ] Trace sampling works correctly

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Prometheus unavailable: metrics buffered
- [ ] Invalid metric name: validation error
- [ ] Alert loop: debounced
- [ ] Notification failure: retried","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-08T13:56:39.384834188-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T21:11:49.627321867-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T21:11:49.627321867-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Metrics and Alerts System: MetricsService (counters, gauges, histograms, snapshots, Prometheus export), AlertService (rules, evaluation, cooldown, acknowledgment), REST endpoints (/metrics, /alerts), and 55 unit tests. All 142 gateway tests pass.","labels":["phase-4","production-ready"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-f9d","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-d18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T14:01:43.605649429-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-fkr","title":"[Epic] Health Check Enhancement","description":"# Epic: Health Check Enhancement

## Background & Problem Statement
The health check endpoint is functional but minimal. It could provide more useful information for operations, debugging, and monitoring.

### Current State Analysis (health.ts:25-31)
```typescript
health.get("/", (c) => {
  return c.json({
    status: "healthy",
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
  });
});
```

The `/health/ready` endpoint is more detailed but basic health is too sparse.

### What's Missing
1. **Version Info**: What version is deployed?
2. **Uptime**: How long has service been running?
3. **Build Info**: Commit SHA, build time
4. **Environment**: Production, staging, etc.
5. **Capabilities**: What features are enabled?

### Industry Standard (Kubernetes/12-Factor)
Health endpoints should support:
- **Liveness**: Is the process alive? (simple, fast)
- **Readiness**: Can it serve traffic? (dependency checks)
- **Startup**: Has initialization completed?

Enhanced health response:
```json
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "version": "1.2.3",
  "commit": "abc123f",
  "uptime": 86400,
  "environment": "production",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-11T...",
  "capabilities": {
    "websocket": true,
    "checkpoints": true,
    "fleet": false
  }
}
```

## Goals
1. **Operational Visibility**: Know what's deployed
2. **Debugging**: Version info in bug reports
3. **Feature Flags**: Advertise capabilities
4. **Monitoring**: Uptime and status metrics

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Health endpoint includes version/commit
- [ ] Uptime tracked and reported
- [ ] Environment name included
- [ ] Capabilities advertised
- [ ] Build info injected at build time
- [ ] Tests verify response shape

## Technical Approach
1. Create build-info.ts with version/commit
2. Track startup time for uptime calculation
3. Define capabilities based on config/env
4. Inject build info during build process
5. Update health route handler

## Build Info Injection
At build time, inject:
```typescript
// build-info.ts (generated)
export const BUILD_INFO = {
  version: "1.2.3",
  commit: "abc123f",
  buildTime: "2024-01-11T10:00:00Z",
  branch: "main"
};
```

## Uptime Tracking
```typescript
const startupTime = Date.now();

function getUptime(): number {
  return Math.floor((Date.now() - startupTime) / 1000);
}
```

## Capabilities Definition
```typescript
const CAPABILITIES = {
  websocket: true,
  checkpoints: process.env.ENABLE_CHECKPOINTS === "true",
  fleet: process.env.ENABLE_FLEET === "true",
  mail: process.env.AGENTMAIL_URL !== undefined,
};
```

## Dependencies
- None, can be done independently

## Risks & Mitigations
- **Information Disclosure**: Version info aids attackers
  - Mitigation: Only expose in internal networks, or use /ready for detailed info
- **Build Complexity**: Need to inject at build time
  - Mitigation: Use simple file generation or env vars","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:02:22.500750924-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:33:42.905144882-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:33:42.905144882-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented build-info.ts service with version, commit, branch, uptime, memory, and capabilities. Updated health.ts routes to include all this info in /health and /health/ready endpoints. All tests passing."}

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-hnv","title":"FEAT: Project Scaffolding and Monorepo Setup","description":"## Overview

Project scaffolding is the foundational infrastructure that enables all other Flywheel Gateway development. This bead establishes the monorepo structure, build tooling, shared package architecture, and CI quality gates that all Phase 1+ features depend on.

## Background & Reasoning

### Why a monorepo?
- **Atomic changes**: cross-package changes are committed together
- **Shared tooling**: single TypeScript + Biome config, consistent scripts
- **Type safety**: local packages type-check together
- **Dev experience**: fast iteration without publish/install cycles

### Why Bun workspaces?
- **Speed**: fast installs + script execution
- **Native TypeScript**: streamlined dev workflow
- **Workspace support**: first-class monorepo support

### Technology Choices

| Technology | Rationale |
|------------|-----------|
| **Bun** | Runtime + package manager + test runner |
| **TypeScript** | Strict type safety, IDE support |
| **Biome** | Unified linting/formatting |
| **Hono** | Backend HTTP framework |
| **Drizzle** | Type-safe ORM for SQLite |
| **React** | UI framework |
| **Tailwind** | Styling system |
| **TanStack Router/Query** | Routing + data fetching |

## Scope & Requirements

### Monorepo Structure

```
flywheel_gateway/
├── apps/
│   ├── gateway/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── routes/
│   │   │   ├── services/
│   │   │   ├── middleware/
│   │   │   ├── db/
│   │   │   ├── ws/
│   │   │   ├── openapi/
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   ├── tests/
│   │   └── package.json
│   └── web/
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── components/
│       │   ├── hooks/
│       │   ├── lib/
│       │   ├── pages/
│       │   ├── stores/
│       │   └── main.tsx
│       ├── tests/
│       └── package.json
├── packages/
│   ├── shared/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── types/
│   │   │   ├── schemas/
│   │   │   ├── commands/
│   │   │   └── utils/
│   │   └── package.json
│   ├── agent-drivers/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── sdk/
│   │   │   ├── acp/
│   │   │   ├── tmux/
│   │   │   └── interface.ts
│   │   └── package.json
│   ├── flywheel-clients/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── agentmail/
│   │   │   ├── bv/
│   │   │   ├── cass/
│   │   │   ├── cm/
│   │   │   └── scanner/
│   │   └── package.json
│   └── test-utils/

```

### Root `package.json` (workspace wiring)

```json
{
  "name": "flywheel-gateway",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"],
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun dev",
    "dev:gateway": "bun dev:gateway",
    "dev:web": "bun dev:web",
    "build": "bun run --filter '*' build",
    "test": "bun test",
    "test:integration": "bun test --filter 'tests/integration/**'",
    "test:e2e": "bunx playwright test",
    "lint": "bun lint",
    "lint:fix": "bun lint:fix",
    "format": "bun format",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "db:generate": "bun db:generate",
    "db:migrate": "bun db:migrate",
    "db:studio": "bun db:studio"
  }
}
```

### TypeScript configuration

- Strict mode enabled
- Base tsconfig shared across packages
- Workspace path aliases for local packages

### Biome configuration

- Lint + format configured for the repo
- CI runs `biome check .` (no auto-write)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] TypeScript compilation succeeds with strict mode
- [ ] All package exports are typed correctly
- [ ] Path aliases resolve correctly
- [ ] Biome linting passes on representative code

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Bun workspaces resolve inter-package dependencies
- [ ] Changes in `packages/shared` reflect in consuming packages
- [ ] `bun dev` starts gateway + web concurrently

### E2E Tests
- [ ] `bun test:e2e` boots the system and validates a minimal smoke flow (gateway health + UI loads)

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Missing dependency / misconfigured workspace fails with actionable error output

### Logging
- [ ] Test logs include a run-level `correlationId` and redact secrets


## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Monorepo structure created with all required directories
- [ ] Root `package.json` configured for Bun workspaces and common scripts
- [ ] Base `tsconfig.json` strict settings applied repo-wide
- [ ] `biome.json` present; `bun lint` and `bun format` are wired
- [ ] Gateway app starts and responds to a health check endpoint
- [ ] Web app starts and renders a placeholder page
- [ ] CI runs: typecheck + lint + unit tests (+ e2e smoke on PR)

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-i6c","title":"Custom Dashboard Builder","description":"## Background

As the Flywheel Gateway platform matures, users need the ability to create personalized views of their agent ecosystem, costs, and activity metrics. A custom dashboard builder enables users to construct tailored monitoring interfaces without requiring engineering intervention, democratizing data visualization across the organization.

## Problem Statement

Currently, users are limited to predefined dashboard layouts that may not align with their specific monitoring needs. Different roles (developers, managers, finance) have distinct requirements for data visualization. Without customization capabilities, users either lack visibility into relevant metrics or must request engineering resources for custom reports.

## Technical Approach

### Core Architecture

The dashboard builder will leverage react-grid-layout for drag-and-drop widget placement, providing a familiar interface similar to tools like Grafana or Datadog dashboards.

```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardBuilder.tsx
interface Dashboard {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description?: string;
  ownerId: string;
  visibility: 'private' | 'team' | 'public';
  teamId?: string;
  layout: DashboardLayout;
  widgets: Widget[];
  refreshInterval: number; // seconds, 0 = manual only
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

interface DashboardLayout {
  columns: number; // typically 12 or 24
  rowHeight: number;
  margin: [number, number];
  containerPadding: [number, number];
}

interface Widget {
  id: string;
  type: WidgetType;
  title: string;
  position: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number };
  config: WidgetConfig;
  dataSource: DataSourceConfig;
}

type WidgetType = 
  | 'metric-card'      // Single KPI with trend
  | 'line-chart'       // Time series
  | 'bar-chart'        // Categorical comparison
  | 'pie-chart'        // Distribution
  | 'table'            // Tabular data
  | 'agent-list'       // Agent status list
  | 'activity-feed'    // Recent events stream
  | 'cost-breakdown'   // Cost visualization
  | 'heatmap'          // Usage patterns
  | 'gauge'            // Progress/capacity
  | 'text'             // Markdown content
  | 'iframe';          // External embeds
```

### Widget Type Specifications

**Metric Cards:**
- Single value display with optional sparkline
- Trend indicator (up/down/neutral with percentage)
- Threshold-based coloring (green/yellow/red)
- Click-through to detailed view

**Charts (Line, Bar, Pie):**
- Built on Chart.js or Recharts
- Configurable time ranges
- Multiple series support
- Legend positioning
- Tooltip customization
- Export as PNG/SVG

**Tables:**
- Sortable columns
- Pagination
- Column visibility toggles
- Row click actions
- Inline search/filter
- CSV export

**Agent Lists:**
- Status indicators (active/idle/error/offline)
- Quick actions (pause, configure, view logs)
- Grouping by team/project/model
- Search and filter

**Activity Feeds:**
- Real-time updates via WebSocket
- Event type filtering
- Expandable details
- Jump to related entity

### Widget Configuration Panel

```typescript
interface WidgetConfigPanel {
  // Common settings
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  refreshInterval?: number;
  
  // Data source
  dataSource: {
    type: 'api' | 'query' | 'static';
    endpoint?: string;
    query?: string; // For custom queries
    filters?: Record<string, any>;
    timeRange?: TimeRange;
  };
  
  // Display settings (type-specific)
  display: {
    colorScheme?: string;
    showLegend?: boolean;
    showGrid?: boolean;
    // ... type-specific options
  };
  
  // Thresholds
  thresholds?: {
    warning?: number;
    critical?: number;
  };
}
```

### Auto-Refresh Configuration

```typescript
interface RefreshConfig {
  globalInterval: number; // Dashboard-wide default
  widgetOverrides: Map<string, number>; // Per-widget overrides
  pauseOnHidden: boolean; // Pause when tab not visible
  pauseOnError: boolean; // Pause widget on consecutive errors
  maxRetries: number; // Before pausing
}
```

### Sharing and Access Control

```typescript
interface DashboardSharing {
  visibility: 'private' | 'team' | 'public';
  teamId?: string; // Required for 'team' visibility
  
  // Granular permissions
  viewers: string[]; // User IDs with read access
  editors: string[]; // User IDs with edit access
  
  // Public options
  publicSlug?: string; // URL-friendly identifier
  requireAuth: boolean; // Require login even for public
  embedEnabled: boolean; // Allow iframe embedding
  embedToken?: string; // For secure embedding
}
```

### Backend Storage

```sql
-- Dashboards table
CREATE TABLE dashboards (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  visibility VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'private',
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  layout JSONB NOT NULL,
  widgets JSONB NOT NULL,
  refresh_interval INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
  public_slug VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
  embed_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  embed_token VARCHAR(64),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Dashboard permissions
CREATE TABLE dashboard_permissions (
  dashboard_id UUID REFERENCES dashboards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  permission VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'view' or 'edit'
  PRIMARY KEY (dashboard_id, user_id)
);

-- Dashboard favorites for quick access
CREATE TABLE dashboard_favorites (
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  dashboard_id UUID REFERENCES dashboards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, dashboard_id)
);
```

### API Endpoints

```typescript
// Dashboard CRUD
GET    /api/v1/dashboards                    // List user's dashboards
POST   /api/v1/dashboards                    // Create dashboard
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Get dashboard
PUT    /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Update dashboard
DELETE /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Delete dashboard
POST   /api/v1/dashboards/:id/duplicate      // Clone dashboard

// Sharing
PUT    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/sharing        // Update sharing settings
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions    // List permissions
POST   /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions    // Add permission
DELETE /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions/:userId

// Widget data
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/widgets/:widgetId/data  // Fetch widget data

// Public access
GET    /api/v1/public/dashboards/:slug       // Public dashboard view
GET    /api/v1/embed/:token                  // Embedded dashboard
```

## File Locations

- apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardBuilder.tsx - Main builder component
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardGrid.tsx - react-grid-layout wrapper
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/widgets/ - Widget components directory
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/WidgetConfigPanel.tsx - Configuration sidebar
- apps/web/src/hooks/useDashboard.ts - Dashboard state management
- apps/gateway/src/services/dashboard.service.ts - Backend service
- apps/gateway/src/controllers/dashboard.controller.ts - API controller
- packages/shared/src/types/dashboard.ts - Shared type definitions

## Dependencies

- react-grid-layout: ^1.4.0 (drag-and-drop grid)
- recharts: ^2.8.0 (charting library)
- @tanstack/react-query: For data fetching and caching
- date-fns: Time range calculations
- react-markdown: For text widgets

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Users can create, edit, and delete custom dashboards
2. Drag-and-drop widget placement works smoothly on desktop browsers
3. All specified widget types are implemented and functional
4. Widget configuration panel allows customizing all relevant settings
5. Auto-refresh works with configurable intervals (15s, 30s, 1m, 5m, 15m, off)
6. Dashboards can be shared with teams or made public
7. Public dashboards are accessible via slug URLs
8. Dashboard state persists correctly across sessions
9. Performance: Dashboard with 20 widgets loads in < 3 seconds
10. Mobile-responsive layout degrades gracefully
11. Widgets display loading states and error handling
12. Export functionality available for charts and tables

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests for widget components
- Integration tests for dashboard CRUD operations
- E2E tests for drag-and-drop interactions
- Performance tests for large dashboards
- Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

## Reference

PLAN.md section 21.8 - Custom Dashboard Builder


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Dashboard layout serialization/deserialization is stable and backward-compatible within a major version
- [ ] Widget registry rejects invalid configs with actionable errors
- [ ] Grid/layout constraints prevent overlapping/invalid placements

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Create/update/delete dashboard via REST and verify persistence + retrieval

### E2E Tests (UI)
- [ ] Drag/drop widgets, resize, save, reload → layout persists and renders correctly

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + dashboardId + widgetCount + action; widget payloads are validated and sanitized
